headline: The connection economy rewards art
song:
Yo, Seth Godin, The Icarus Deception—hear the shift:
The connection economy rewards art, not just the thrift of safety gifts.
Industrial age paid for compliance; this age pays for who you move—
Flip it for today, make the connection prove.
[Chorus]
The connection economy rewards art—people, not only units sold,
Who you change, who you wake, who you hold with something bold.
Compliance was the old paycheck; connection is the new rent,
Art is the scarce thing when stuff is infinite and spent.
Ship the work that makes someone feel seen and changed,
Not the work that only stays arranged.
Connection economy—art is the currency—
Make it, send it, urgency.
[Verse 1]
Godin said the industrial economy trained us to fit, follow, finish the form,
Factory rules: don't make a storm, don't be warm, don't transform.
Then the connection economy arrived—attention, trust, tribes that choose,
Art became the scarce resource when commodities lose.
Art here isn't only paint—it's the generous act that creates a bond,
The thing only a human caring hard can respond beyond.
Icarus was told don't fly too high; Godin flips the myth for makers:
The real danger is flying too low for the stakers of safe favors.
History: mass production rewarded sameness at scale,
Now sameness is free online—connection is the tale that sells without a sale's old jail.
True fans, tiny circles, work that matters to someone specific,
The connection economy is specific, not only prolific.
Godin teaches: your job is to make art that connects on purpose daily,
Not wait for a boss to stamp you as creatively.
Reward follows the human who risks being remarkable and kind in public,
Art as the product of the republic of attention.
[Chorus]
[Verse 2]
Modern day apply it: AI can spit a thousand safe posts by noon—
Connection still rewards the human cut that lands like a moon.
Side project? Don't optimize only for reach graphs cold,
Optimize for one stranger who says "this changed how I hold my work."
Job search: portfolio that shows judgment and care beats keyword spam,
Connection economy hires the human who can jam with a team, not only a exam.
Teacher on TikTok or classroom: art is the lesson that connects a kid to meaning,
Not only the worksheet cleaning of standards gleaming.
Founder: ship the feature that makes one cohort gasp "finally,"
Not the roadmap checkbox that flatters the vanity of many.
Phone-free hour titled this insight—show up even if mood disappears—
Make one connecting artifact: note, demo, draft that steers a real someone near.
Tomorrow: book that hour, put something human in the world that could only come from you,
Connection is the due.
[Bridge]
Stuff is cheap; care is expensive...
Art is expensive on purpose...
Connection doesn't scale like a factory line...
It scales like trust over time.
[Verse 3 / Outro]
So own it: the connection economy rewards art—Godin's map for the age,
Stop hiding in industrial cages.
Book one phone-free hour, title it connection, make one true thing for one real person,
Even if the mood goes missing—show as the version.
When feeds reward noise and jobs reward gray compliance still,
Practice art that connects on the hill of tomorrow's will.
One human moved by something you shipped—that's the metric that counts—
Connection economy—mounts.
Art that binds—work that matters.
Connect—then scatter the safe.

headline: Safety is often the riskiest choice
song:
Yo, Seth Godin, The Icarus Deception—invert the fear:
Safety is often the riskiest choice—hear it clear.
Playing small feels safe till the market, soul, and craft go stale—
Flip it for today, risk the trail.
[Chorus]
Safety is often the riskiest choice—paradox loud,
Hide from failure and you fail by never leaving the crowd.
Wings too low melt slower but you never touch the sun of meaning,
Safe lane is a quiet demeaning.
The risky path is staying average in a world that needs your art,
Safety as risk—start.
Choose the scar of trying over the rust of hiding—
Safe is sliding.
[Verse 1]
Godin retells Icarus: the warning was also don't fly too low,
Industrial culture only sold the high-flight woe.
Bosses, schools, parents trained risk as the enemy of a good life,
He says comfort without contribution is a knife to the real life.
Safety feels rational—stable job, approved taste, no public miss—
But when art is what is needed, safety is the abyss of irrelevance.
History of careers: people who never shipped the scary work got replaced by those who did,
Or by tools that ate the timid mid-grid.
Godin's lineage: tribes, permission marketing, linchpin—all push the same bone,
You are not a cog; safe cogs get outsourced alone.
Risk here isn't reckless gambling—it's emotional exposure for connection,
Putting work where judgment can land without protection of forever draft mode.
The riskiest choice is a life optimized only to avoid embarrassment,
Because the bill comes due as emptiness and vanishing market rent.
So reframe: the brave daily act is often the safer long game for meaning and skill,
Safety theater kills.
[Chorus]
[Verse 2]
Modern day apply it: don't only automate the safe tasks with AI and call it growth,
Ship the judgment call only you can own—both.
Stay in the role that bores you because "benefits"—name the real risk of soul and skill decay,
Update the résumé of courage, not only the 401k of gray.
Creator afraid to post the half-formed idea: safety is the algorithm forgetting you,
Risk is one honest post that teaches a crew.
Student picks only easy electives for GPA armor—risky for a mind that never strains,
Take one hard class that rewires the brains.
Parent modeling only safe opinions at dinner—risky for kids who never see dissent done kind,
Model a scary true line.
Phone-free hour: sit with the work that scares you, not the inbox that soothes,
Safety's risk exposed when you refuse the smooth.
Tomorrow: book the hour, do the uncomfortable send, apply, or share—
Risk on purpose with care.
[Bridge]
Fear dresses up as prudence...
Prudence without contribution is a sentence...
Wings low feel warm till the years freeze...
Choose heat that frees.
[Verse 3 / Outro]
So name the trap: safety is often the riskiest choice—Godin's Icarus corrected,
Fly the work you've neglected.
Phone-free hour tomorrow—title it the scary ship—show up mood or not,
One action that could fail in public on the spot.
When culture sells insurance for every feeling and feed,
Buy the premium of trying instead of the cheap of only agreed.
Send the draft, book the call, start the thing that could embarrass and elevate—
Risky safety—negate.
Wings up—smart risk.
Safe small—no more of this.

headline: Fly closer to the sun—make scarier work
song:
Yo, Seth Godin, The Icarus Deception—altitude up:
Fly closer to the sun—make scarier work—fill the cup.
Not crash-and-burn ego—heat that means you finally care enough to risk—
Flip it for today, scare yourself on purpose, brisk.
[Chorus]
Fly closer to the sun—make scarier work—that's the ask,
If it doesn't scare a little, it might be a mask.
Scarier means more personal, more generous, more likely to fail in public view,
That's where connection breaks through.
Low flight is comfort; high flight is art with sweat on the brow,
Closer to the sun—now.
Make the work that could actually matter or sting—
Scary is the wing.
[Verse 1]
Godin uses Icarus to free makers from the cult of permanent caution,
The sun is intensity: stakes, honesty, emotional motion.
Scarier work isn't cruelty or chaos—it's work that could be rejected because it's real,
The opposite of wallpaper you can conceal.
Industrial training said stay in the middle lane of taste and role,
He says the middle lane is crowded and soul-poor as a goal.
History of work that lasts: someone risked looking foolish for a clearer truth,
From bold ads to bold books—scare was the youth of the proof.
Linchpin thinking lives here: indispensable people do the emotional labor of art,
Scarier because you can't hide behind a chart of "just following."
Make scarier work means raise the personal stakes of the next draft,
Not the budget— the craft of saying what you actually mean in the aft of polish.
Godin wants daily practice of slight more courage, not one cinematic leap only,
Altitude in reps—lonely then less lonely.
Sun is the audience that can feel you; fly toward that heat with skill,
Not toward vanity's spill.
[Chorus]
[Verse 2]
Modern day apply it: rewrite the launch email without corporate fog—name the fear it solves,
Scarier copy that actually involves a human pulse.
AI will draft the safe version in ten seconds flat—your job is the scarier edit,
The line that could get a reply or a hit of critique—commit.
Performance review: ask for the hard feedback, propose the ambitious project first,
Fly closer than the "keep my head down" thirst.
Side hustle: ship the weird niche that might only hit two hundred true fans,
Scarier than another generic brand that blandly stands.
Kid's recital or science fair: encourage the ambitious messy version, not only safe ribbon,
Closer to the sun of trying, not the shade of given.
Seven mornings: read Godin's line, then two-minute matching action—
One scarier sentence sent, one ask, one creation fraction.
[Bridge]
Comfort is a dim room that feels like home...
The sun is where the work becomes known...
Scare is information—you're near something true...
Fly a degree or two.
[Verse 3 / Outro]
So fly closer to the sun—make scarier work—Godin's altitude drill for the week,
Two minutes each dawn: one act that makes your voice squeak then speak.
Read the line, then send, ask, publish, or cut the coward phrase,
Scarier by a notch for seven days.
When the feed rewards safe templates and jobs reward gray calm,
Be the person who adds heat without false alarm of pure recklessness.
Seven days, seven scarier inches—stack them into a different career weather—
Sunward—together with skill.
Scare means you care—ship there.
Closer—declare.

headline: Industrial permission rules are outdated
song:
Yo, Seth Godin, The Icarus Deception—tear the old pass:
Industrial permission rules are outdated—don't wait for the class.
Boss, gatekeeper, degree, "who said you could"—that map expired—
Flip it for today, permission rewired.
[Chorus]
Industrial permission rules are outdated—stop the queue,
You don't need a hall pass to make something true.
Factory world needed compliance tickets to move a part,
Connection world needs you to start.
Waiting for approval is a habit from another century's floor,
Outdated rules—ignore the more that keep you poor of impact.
Pick the work, ship the art, ask forgiveness less than you invent—
Permission reinvented.
[Verse 1]
Godin maps the industrial age: scarce slots, managers as gods of yes,
You waited to be picked for the desk that blesses.
Schools mirrored factories—raise hand, get grade, get job if you obey the chime,
Permission was the currency of time.
Then tools, networks, and audiences fragmented—anyone can publish, sell, teach, build,
The old permission cartel got killed as the only guild.
Outdated doesn't mean laws and ethics vanished—means creative and career gates moved,
You can start without the velvet groove of institutional love.
History: bloggers, indie game makers, open-source leads, direct-to-fan musicians,
They stopped waiting for the permission physicians of culture.
Godin's linchpin and Icarus share blood: stop requiring a stamp to care hard,
Industrial manners are a shard of a broken yard.
The habit to fight is "I can't until they let me"—name it, cut it half this week,
While you practice Icarus technique.
Rules of safety, sameness, and "stay in your lane" were optimized for factories not for art,
Update the chart.
[Chorus]
[Verse 2]
Modern day apply it: don't wait for the company innovation committee to bless your prototype,
Build the small proof on nights—invite later, not as the only rite.
School project or research: start the experiment before the perfect approval chain finishes,
Within ethics—outdated is the endless spin of wishes.
Creator career: platforms let you post without a label A&R nod,
Industrial permission was the old god—now the audience is the squad.
Corporate designer waiting for brand to "allow" a better pattern—ship a sandbox demo,
Outdated gate, modern memo: show don't only beg though.
Habit that fights this: refreshing email for a yes before any draft exists—
Cut that habit in half—make first, then list the risks.
Tomorrow: name the waiting habit, cut it fifty percent, practice one unpermitted-but-honest start,
Godin's art of the heart without the old chart.
[Bridge]
Permission was scarce when tools were scarce...
Tools got free; courage stayed the tax...
Outdated rules live rent-free in your head...
Evict them—instead.
[Verse 3 / Outro]
So industrial permission rules are outdated—act like the calendar says now,
Godin's broken-gate vow.
Name one waiting habit this week, cut it in half, start the thing without the stamp,
One concrete begin—lamp on, hands damp with real work.
When jobs and schools still perform industrial theater of raise-hand culture,
Play the modern game: ship, then structure.
No more "who allowed me"—allow yourself inside ethics and care—
Outdated—tear.
Start without the pass.
Permission—your task.

headline: Pick yourself
song:
Yo, Seth Godin, The Icarus Deception—shortest thunder:
Pick yourself—stop waiting to be chosen under.
The gatekeepers are busy, wrong, or gone—your turn to step—
Flip it for today, self-select.
[Chorus]
Pick yourself—don't rent your worth to a stranger's nod,
The stage is open more than the old gods of A&R and board.
Waiting to be picked is a career of maybe and mute,
Picking yourself is the route.
Ship, apply, host, teach, launch—without the fairy tale invite,
Pick yourself tonight.
You are the permission slip—sign it in ink—
Pick yourself—think.
[Verse 1]
Godin hammered this across books: the world changed from "get picked" to "pick you,"
Record labels, publishers, bosses used to be the few.
Now the bottleneck is your willingness to be seen doing the work imperfect,
Not a scarcity of microphones—scarce is the circuit of courage perfect.
Pick yourself means initiate: the newsletter, the product, the hard conversation, the art show in a cafe,
Not the fantasy that someone discovers your genius in the shade.
History of careers that look overnight: years of self-picking in public quiet,
Then the "sudden" riot of attention on the diet of shipped try-it.
Industrial story sold lottery tickets of being chosen by talent scouts,
Godin sells the daily scout in your own house.
Habit that fights pick-yourself: refreshing applications only, never building proof of work,
Or waiting for a mentor to assign your worth.
Cut that habit in half this week while you practice Icarus—
One self-pick act that sticks to us.
Picking yourself isn't narcissism—it's responsibility for your contribution,
Adult art distribution.
[Chorus]
[Verse 2]
Modern day apply it: stop only applying to dream jobs—ship a public project that is the interview,
Pick yourself into the view.
AI tools lower the cost of a first version—pick yourself to publish v1 this week,
Not v17 in secret for the meek.
Want a podcast, club, study group? Schedule the first date, invite five people, start,
No celebrity cohost required for the heart of the art.
Internal mobility at work: propose the role by doing a slice of it already,
Pick yourself into the steady of demonstrated ready.
Dating, friendship, collaboration same physics—initiate more than you lurk,
Pick yourself out of the murk of endless scroll research.
Habit to cut: "when they notice me" fantasies—replace with calendar blocks of making,
Self-pick is a making.
Tomorrow: name the wait-to-be-chosen habit, half it, do one picked-myself move before bed—
Godin's line lived instead of only read.
[Bridge]
Chosen feels magical; picking feels like work...
Work is how magic gets to smirk...
Nobody is coming with a crown on a tray...
Pick—then they may.
[Verse 3 / Outro]
So pick yourself—Godin's blunt medicine for the permission hangover,
Icarus sober.
Cut the waiting habit in half this week—one launch, one ask, one post, one prototype public,
Self-picked—not only subject to the republic of luck.
When culture still sells "get discovered" as the plot,
Write a different plot: you select your shot.
Sign your own permission, ship before you're comfortable, own the awkward first—
Pick yourself—thirst for the real.
You choose—you move.
Picked—by you.

headline: Ship imperfect work that connects
song:
Yo, Seth Godin, The Icarus Deception—dock the myth of polish forever:
Ship imperfect work that connects—now or never.
Perfect is a stall; connection needs a date on the calendar—
Flip it for today, ship the unfinished banner.
[Chorus]
Ship imperfect work that connects—done beats divine,
Ugly that moves someone beats pretty that never signs the line.
Imperfect is honest; hidden perfect is a fantasy vault,
Ship so the real can revolt against the fault of delay.
Connect first, refine next—in public enough to learn,
Ship the burn.
Art in motion, not art in museum of your hard drive—
Imperfect—alive.
[Verse 1]
Godin teaches shipping as a moral and practical act for artists of every job,
Work that never ships can't connect—it's a soliloquy for a mob of one.
Imperfect isn't sloppy ethics or cruelty—it's accepting version one will limp,
And limping in the world beats sprinting in a dream gym.
Industrial quality control said "don't ship till flawless" for physical defects,
Connection work dies under that same dialect.
History of Godin's own practice: daily blogging, small risks, visible drafts of thought,
Shipping as the gym where taste is bought with reps, not only taught.
Icarus angle: fear of melting makes people never leave the ground of revision,
He wants heat—ship into the sun of opinion.
Connection happens at the point of contact, not at the point of theoretical peak quality,
So schedule contact, then iterate reality.
The enemy is endless buffing to avoid the feeling of being judged,
Ship imperfect—be begrudged and then adjusted.
Generous artists ship because someone needs the help now, not after your ego is safe,
That's the faith.
[Chorus]
[Verse 2]
Modern day apply it: hit publish on the Notion guide at eighty percent clarity,
Invite three users—connection first, polish later as charity to the work.
AI makes perfect-looking drafts cheap—don't let glossy fake-finish delay the human test,
Ship the weird useful mess.
Designer: Figma link to stakeholders before the pixels are sacred,
Imperfect review is how the product gets hydrated.
Student paper: office hours with a rough outline beats all-nighter perfect silence,
Ship the thinking for alliance with feedback.
Parent apology or hard talk: imperfect honest now beats perfect script never spoken,
Connection is the token.
Seven mornings: read Godin's line, two-minute action—ship one small imperfect thing,
Email, commit, post, send the ring of "here it is."
[Bridge]
Perfectionism wears a craftsman mask...
Often it's fear in a flask...
Connection is a handshake, not a statue...
Ship—then match you.
[Verse 3 / Outro]
So ship imperfect work that connects—Godin's daily courage prescription,
Icarus with a shipping addiction on purpose.
Seven days, two minutes after the line: one send that isn't ready-for-museum,
Ready-for-human—that's the medium.
When platforms and jobs reward only highlight reels of finish,
Be the person who connects mid-build and diminishes the fear of diminish.
Imperfect out the door tomorrow morning before coffee cools—
Connect—those are the rules.
Ship ugly—touch true.
Imperfect—through.

headline: Build true fans, not mass indifference
song:
Yo, Seth Godin, The Icarus Deception—audience math rewritten:
Build true fans, not mass indifference—quit the smitten with vanity metrics.
A thousand who care beat a million who scroll past cold—
Flip it for today, grow the fold that holds.
[Chorus]
Build true fans, not mass indifference—depth over spray,
People who would miss you if you went away.
Mass indifference is easy likes and empty reach charts,
True fans show up when the art starts.
Small circle, real connection, work made for someone specific—
Not everyone—terrific.
Godin's tribe logic lives here—earn the few who lean in—
True fans—begin.
[Verse 1]
Godin long argued against mass-market fantasy for most creators and startups,
You don't need everybody—you need the people who get the cuts of your sharp.
True fans buy, share, defend, wait, fund, forgive the imperfect ship,
Mass indifference applauds politely in the abstract and skips.
Icarus connection economy: art for someone beats wallpaper for everyone,
Indifference is the tax of aiming at the sun of pure scale too soon.
History: independent musicians, authors, indie SaaS—survivors built the thousand true,
Not the Super Bowl ad as the only glue.
Kevin Kelly's "1000 true fans" rhymes with Godin's tribes—same family of insight,
Specificity is the light.
Habit that fights this: posting only for algorithmic generalism, never for a named human need,
Cut that habit in half—plant a different seed.
Build means consistent generous work for a defined circle, not a one-hit chase,
Fans are grown in the base of trust, not bought in a race of paid face.
Mass indifference feels like success in dashboards and death in the bank of meaning,
Choose the leaning.
[Chorus]
[Verse 2]
Modern day apply it: newsletter of eight hundred openers who reply beats ten thousand ghost subs,
True fans in the inbox hubs.
TikTok/Reels: stop only chasing general viral—make the series for the niche that comments with stories,
Indifference is the glory of empty forries of views.
Startup: ten design partners who use weekly > press hit that converts nobody,
True fans as the lobby of product.
Teacher: twelve students deeply changed > performative school PR for the district,
Build the fan of learning, not the indifference of metrics.
Habit to cut: vanity refresh of follower count as the morning prayer—
Half that habit—spend the minutes on one fan care: reply, improve, share.
Tomorrow: name the mass-chase habit, cut half, do one true-fan act—personal note, niche post, call—
Godin's ball in your hall.
[Bridge]
Everyone is nobody when you aim at everyone...
Someone is everyone when you aim at the right someone...
Indifference scales; love concentrates...
Build where it resonates.
[Verse 3 / Outro]
So build true fans, not mass indifference—Godin's audience discipline,
Icarus with a smaller, hotter ignition.
Cut the mass-habit in half this week; invest those hours in the people who already care,
One reply, one exclusive draft, one show-up there.
When platforms pay you to chase indifferent crowds forever,
Opt into the tribe that makes the work better and never say never to small.
True fans first—scale only as a side effect of depth—
Not the other theft of self.
Few who lean—beat the sea of shrug.
True fans—hug the craft, not the shrug.

headline: Art is voluntary hardship for meaning
song:
Yo, Seth Godin, The Icarus Deception—define the labor:
Art is voluntary hardship for meaning—not only glamor.
You choose the hard thing because connection is worth the sweat—
Flip it for today, volunteer the debt of effort met.
[Chorus]
Art is voluntary hardship for meaning—choose the weight,
Nobody forces the scary draft; you open the gate.
Hardship without meaning is just grind; meaning without hardship is wish,
Art is both—the dish.
Voluntary: you could quit to comfort; you don't, because someone needs the work,
Hardship as a perk of purpose, not a quirk of pain for show.
Meaning is the why that makes the hard make sense—
Art—intense.
[Verse 1]
Godin strips art of precious gallery-only costume,
Art is the act of making change happen through emotional labor and costume of risk.
Voluntary hardship: you accept rejection, revision, exposure, discipline on purpose,
Because industrial comfort won't produce the surface of real connection.
It's not suffering cosplay—it's adult choice to do what is difficult and generous,
When easy would pay the rent with less of us.
History of any craft lineage: hours nobody applauded, choices to stay in the problem,
Voluntary—because the meaning problem is human, not only a job-em.
Icarus flies toward heat voluntarily; the myth's courage is chosen altitude,
Godin wants that chosen in your calendar's multitude of small days.
Habit that fights this: only doing work when inspired or easy dopamine hits—
Cut that habit in half—show up for the grit of meaning fits.
Hardship includes awkward feedback, boring practice, saying no to status games,
All voluntary if you claim the aims of art.
Meaning answers: who is better because you did this hard thing today,
Without that, hardship is just display.
[Chorus]
[Verse 2]
Modern day apply it: writing the docs nobody claps for but the team needs—art if meaning-led,
Voluntary hardship in the shed of real work, not only the stage of the fed.
AI can remove some toil—don't remove the meaningful hard of judgment and taste,
Keep the hardship that makes the work chaste and placed for humans.
Training for a race, learning an instrument, building a course: same Godin frame,
Hard on purpose for a name of change.
Parenting patience on a rough night is voluntary hardship for meaning too,
Art of the human crew.
Habit to cut: quitting at first friction while calling it "self-care" every time—
Sometimes care is continuing the climb—discern the line.
Tomorrow: name the ease-only habit, cut half, schedule one voluntary hard block for meaning—
No posting—just the leaning into the weight.
[Bridge]
Comfort is default biology...
Art is chosen ideology of effort...
Hardship becomes holy when the why is clear...
Volunteer—stay near.
[Verse 3 / Outro]
So art is voluntary hardship for meaning—live Godin's definition this week,
Not slogans—specific cheek-to-desk technique.
Cut the ease-only habit in half; book hardship that serves a person or a craft truth,
Twenty-five or sixty minutes of meaning-proof.
When culture sells either hustle porn without meaning or comfort porn without contribution,
Choose voluntary hard that builds distribution of care.
One hard meaningful act tomorrow you could have skipped—don't skip—
Art is that grip.
Choose the weight—make it matter.
Voluntary—latter of the day done better.

headline: Ignore structures that only grant permission
song:
Yo, Seth Godin, The Icarus Deception—filter the buildings:
Ignore structures that only grant permission—not real ceilings.
If a structure's only power is "you may" or "you may not" without making value—
Flip it for today, don't worship the gate's residue.
[Chorus]
Ignore structures that only grant permission—see the bluff,
Some gates exist to feel important, not to make the work enough.
Real structures teach, fund, protect, distribute, improve the craft,
Permission-only structures are a raft of ego, not a craft.
Walk around, build beside, stop kneeling for a stamp that adds no skill—
Ignore the thrill of waiting on the hill of no.
Godin's pick-yourself energy with a systems eye—
Permission mills—pass by.
[Verse 1]
Godin distinguishes institutions that enable from those that only ration status,
Permission-only: committees that never ship, clubs that only exclude with malice of prestige.
Industrial age multiplied permission structures—unions of taste, HR theater, guild cosplay,
Some still useful; many just delay.
Ignoring isn't breaking laws or harming people—it's refusing to treat "no badge" as "no art,"
You can make without their chart.
History of indie movements: when official channels only granted permission to the already connected,
Makers built parallel connected paths and defected from the wait.
Icarus says fly; permission-only towers say wait on the tarmac forever,
Godin says the tower often has no weather of real value—just lever of control.
Restate short: skip gates that only say yes/no without helping the work grow—
Then a 25-minute block that obeys—no posting, only go.
Structures worth engaging: ones that give critique, capital, distribution, safety, learning,
Permission-only is just yearning control with a meeting.
Train your eye: does this structure make better art possible or only make you smaller till they smile,
Ignore the latter mile.
[Chorus]
[Verse 2]
Modern day apply it: industry award circuit that only launders prestige—maybe skip this year,
Make the work; ignore the auctioneer of permission.
Internal "innovation board" that never funds, only vetoes—prototype outside its gaze,
Show results later in a blaze of done, not request-phase.
Academic or corporate letterhead worship: useful sometimes, often a permission idol,
Ship the paper, the tool, the model—idol idle.
Social platforms' unofficial courts of taste—don't need every micro-influencer's blessing,
Ignore structures that only dress as assessing.
Creator collabs that are only clout-permission exchanges with no craft exchange—
Walk from the strange of empty range.
Tomorrow: twelve words max restate, 25-minute work block that obeys—no posting—
One real output while permission structures keep roasting empty.
[Bridge]
Not every no is wisdom...
Some no is just a system...
Value-adding structures deserve respect and time...
Permission cosplay—decline.
[Verse 3 / Outro]
So ignore structures that only grant permission—Godin's clear-eyed rebel with a calendar,
Icarus doesn't rent altitude from a janitor of status.
Restate in twelve words, run twenty-five minutes of making that needs no stamp,
No posting—hands in the damp of actual work.
When every feed invents new permission priests of taste and brand safety theater,
Build beside them—better.
One block tomorrow that proves you didn't need their yes to exist—
Ignore—persist.
Gates of only maybe—walk around.
Make—on solid ground.

headline: Make the work only you can make
song:
Yo, Seth Godin, The Icarus Deception—close the arc personal:
Make the work only you can make—that's the kernel.
Not the generic best practices clone—the signature only your life can sign—
Flip it for today, draw your line.
[Chorus]
Make the work only you can make—unique isn't a brand filter,
It's your taste, scars, people, and grit in the mixture.
Anyone can copy a template; no one can copy your particular care,
Only-you is rare.
Connection economy pays for the human fingerprint on the gift,
Not the shift of pure replaceable thrift.
Only you—then ship it toward someone true—
That's the Icarus due.
[Verse 1]
Godin's whole project points here: art is personal responsibility made visible,
If it's fully generic, it's industrial product, not the tribal signal.
Only you can make doesn't mean isolated genius myth—means your combination of inputs,
Lineage, pain, humor, hometown, mentors, failed experiments as sprints.
Replaceable work gets automated, outsourced, averaged into mush,
Irreplaceable work connects because it couldn't be another person's brush.
History of linchpins: the nurse who redesigned the handoff, the teacher with the odd method that works,
The designer who refuses the bland that lurks in every deck.
Icarus flying low is doing the work anyone in your seat would do the same,
Flying toward the sun is signing your name with risk of blame.
Habit against this: copying the top creator's exact cadence till you vanish,
Or waiting to be original before you practice—both a banish of self.
Book a phone-free hour titled the insight—show up even if mood disappears—
Make the only-you thing with no mirrors of peers for sixty clear.
The test: if your name fell off, would someone still feel it was yours in the bones,
Only-you tones.
[Chorus]
[Verse 2]
Modern day apply it: AI can draft the average email, deck, and caption—
Your job is the only-you judgment, story, and passion fraction.
Portfolio: one piece that couldn't have come from a bootcamp prompt pack,
Personal constraint, local problem, weird stack.
Manager: the one-on-one style only you run that grows people—that's art of leadership,
Not the HR template clip.
Parent: the ritual only your family would invent—connection economy at home,
Only-you as the poem of the day, not a chrome of influencers' roam.
Side project: mash your two distant skills—nursing + comics, code + kitchen, sports + systems—
Intersection is the only-you prism.
Phone-free hour tomorrow: title it "only I can," produce one artifact without comparing tabs,
Mood optional—show up—grab the abs of discipline.
[Bridge]
Templates scale; souls don't photocopy well...
Only-you is a responsibility, not a spell of ego...
The world has enough averages...
Give what only you averages into something new.
[Verse 3 / Outro]
So make the work only you can make—Godin's send-off for the Icarus reader,
Be the author, not only the repeater.
Book the phone-free hour, title it honest, leave with one thing that bears your mark,
Even if the mood goes dark—still spark.
When every tool makes everyone sound the same by default,
Default is the insult—assault it with your fault lines and strengths.
Only-you shipped tomorrow is the whole book in one act—
Connect—exact.
Your work—your sun—your flight.
Only you—tonight.

headline: Ignore everybody—selectively
song:
Yo, Hugh MacLeod, Ignore Everybody—open the cartoon knife:
Ignore everybody—selectively—that's the creative life.
Not hermit rage—filters for noise so the idea can grow teeth—
Flip it for today, choose who gets underneath.
[Chorus]
Ignore everybody—selectively—not a blanket mute,
Ignore the fear salesmen, the committee of cute absolute.
Hear the few who love the work and tell hard truth with care,
Ignore the rest of the air.
Selective deafness is a craft skill, not a tantrum pose,
Protect the early rose.
MacLeod's law: good ideas die in crowds of polite advice—
Select—then thrice think twice.
[Verse 1]
MacLeod drew on the back of business cards, built a voice by not sanding every edge for the room,
Ignore Everybody is a field manual against death by consensus gloom.
Selectively matters: mentors, customers, partners still count when they earn the mic,
Everybody else is weather—you don't argue with every spike of rain.
Good ideas have lonely childhoods—crowds want familiar, safe, already proven,
So early work needs a cone of silence from the oven of opinions.
History of makers: the ones who asked the whole office too soon got redesigned into beige,
Selective ignore is the stage of courage and the gauge of taste.
He isn't anti-feedback—he's anti-random feedback as governance,
Gate your ears like you gate your time—same relevance.
Habit that fights this: sending every draft to the group chat for dopamine votes—
Cut that habit in half—fewer throats, clearer notes from the right folks.
Ignore the people who only protect their comfort from your change,
Listen to people who protect the work's range.
That's selective—surgical—kind when needed, deaf when the noise is greed for control,
MacLeod's patrol.
[Chorus]
[Verse 2]
Modern day apply it: mute the productivity guru pile-on when you're mid-making,
Ignore everybody selling fear of missing the latest AI tooling as moral failing.
Design review: three trusted reviewers max, not the whole Slack channel circus,
Selective ignore is a purpose.
Family group chat opinions on your career pivot—love them, don't obey all,
Select whose map you install.
Student project: teacher + one sharp peer, not fifteen group-edits into mush,
Ignore everybody-ish with a brush of criteria: craft, care, not status flush.
Habit to cut: refreshing comments as compass—half it this week while you practice the book,
One ignored thread that would have shook your nerve for no good look.
Tomorrow: name the over-listening habit, cut half, make one move without a poll—
Selective ignore as the goal.
[Bridge]
Everybody has an opinion; few have skin in your game...
Crowds average toward the same...
Select the ears that make you braver and sharper...
Ignore the harper of fear.
[Verse 3 / Outro]
So ignore everybody—selectively—MacLeod's opening commandment with a scalpel,
Not a shutdown of all help.
Cut the habit of crowd-sourcing your courage in half this week,
Practice Ignore Everybody with technique: list three voices in, mute the rest mid-seek.
When feeds and workplaces are opinion firehoses twenty-four seven,
Selective ignore is oxygen from heaven for the work.
One decision tomorrow made without a public vote—own it clean—
Selective—keen.
Ears open to few—heart open to craft.
Ignore—on behalf of the draft.

headline: Good ideas have lonely childhoods
song:
Yo, Hugh MacLeod, Ignore Everybody—lonely nursery truth:
Good ideas have lonely childhoods—protect the youth of the new.
They look stupid early, quiet, friendless in the market of cool—
Flip it for today, parent the idea through school.
[Chorus]
Good ideas have lonely childhoods—don't panic at the silence,
Early loneliness isn't proof of failure—it's the science of difference.
Crowds arrive late; the childhood is yours to guard and feed,
Keep drawing, keep the seed.
If it were obvious and popular at birth, it wouldn't be the edge,
Lonely is the ledge where originals pledge.
MacLeod: sit with the quiet—don't kill it for company—
Lonely childhood—destiny of the good.
[Verse 1]
MacLeod watched ideas die because makers need applause too early,
A good idea in diapers can't survive a focus group of the surly and the safe.
Lonely childhood means few understand, fewer cheer, metrics look embarrassing,
Your job is nursery care, not a press conference in the crib dressing.
He kept making cartoons when the "serious" world shrugged—
Loneliness as the climate where a voice gets rugged.
History of products, art, science: early days look like toys or mistakes,
Until the childhood breaks into adolescence of traction, then the world remakes its takes.
Ignore Everybody exists so you don't abandon the child when the room is empty,
Empty room is often the plenty of space to grow the entity.
Not every lonely idea is good—discernment still applies—
But every good idea spends time uncanonized before it flies.
So build tolerance for unread posts, unbought sketches, unjoined waitlists at first,
Lonely is the thirst test of whether you mean the work or only the burst of attention.
Parent the idea: feed it hours, protect it from premature committee adoption,
MacLeod's option.
[Chorus]
[Verse 2]
Modern day apply it: your weird app idea with twelve users isn't dead—it's a toddler,
Make one small finished thing that only exists if lonely childhoods are true—bolder.
Ship a cartoon, a feature, a chapter that nobody asked for yet,
Proof you can midwife without a duet of hype.
AI makes it easy to generate fifty "ideas"—lonely childhood applies to the one you commit to raise,
Not the pile of phrase-maze.
Startup metrics dashboard screaming red in month two—expect lonely, design for learning,
Not only for the yearning of vanity turning.
Kid with an odd invention: don't shame the loneliness of no classmates getting it yet,
Protect the childhood of the concept.
Tomorrow: one finished small artifact—card, post, prototype—that leans into being early and alone,
From Ignore Everybody—own the zone.
[Bridge]
Popularity is a late guest...
Childhood is the test of whether you'll stay...
Lonely isn't loveless if you love the work...
Keep the quirk.
[Verse 3 / Outro]
So good ideas have lonely childhoods—MacLeod's lullaby with grit,
Stop demanding a stadium for a skit still in script.
Make one small finished thing tomorrow that could only live if this line is true,
A lonely-but-real debut.
When culture only celebrates the viral adolescent idea already loud,
Be the parent in the quiet crowd of one.
Finish the small thing, put it where a future fan could find it—no committee bath—
Lonely path.
Raise the idea—don't rush the years.
Childhood—cheers later; now—perseveres.

headline: Sex-and-cash theory: balance love projects and money projects
song:
Yo, Hugh MacLeod, Ignore Everybody—two engines, one life:
Sex-and-cash theory—balance love projects and money projects—no endless strife.
Love work feeds the soul; cash work feeds the rent—both legitimate—
Flip it for today, dual stack, don't resent the split.
[Chorus]
Sex-and-cash theory—love projects and money projects in balance,
Not pure romantic poverty, not pure sold-out talents.
Cash without love dries the artist; love without cash breaks the spine,
Balance the line.
One pays the bills with honesty; one pays the spirit with fire,
MacLeod's dual-hire for your time.
Ignore the purity police on either side—
Balance—ride both tides.
[Verse 1]
MacLeod's sex-and-cash theory (his blunt labels): work you love like desire, work that brings money,
Healthy creative life often needs the tandem, not a fantasy of only honey.
Pure cash grind with zero love projects and you become a hollow vendor of hours,
Pure love with zero cash plan and the art dies in the showers of stress and unpaid powers.
He drew the tension so freelancers and office creatives could stop lying to themselves,
Balance is strategy, not a secret of elves.
History of working artists: day rates and night canvases, client logos and personal comics,
The body of work includes both economics.
Ignore Everybody isn't "quit your job tomorrow for purity"—it's smarter allocation,
Know which project is sex, which is cash, stop confusing the station.
Habit that fights balance: shame about paid work or shame about unpaid passion—
Cut that habit in half—own both as ration of a real career.
Love projects keep your taste alive so cash work doesn't make you mediocre forever,
Cash projects buy time so love can weather the lonely childhoods better.
Name the mix on paper—hours, energy, goals—then adjust like a pilot,
MacLeod's violet middle, not the extremist diet.
[Chorus]
[Verse 2]
Modern day apply it: full-time job is cash; weekend game or newsletter is love—calendar both,
Don't let Slack eat the oath to the love growth.
Freelance: separate invoices from personal sketch series—both on the board,
Balance isn't vibes—it's the scored hours you can afford.
AI automates some cash tasks—reinvest freed minutes into love projects deliberately,
Don't only fill with more cash indefinitely.
Student: scholarship hustle (cash-ish) + the weird film only you want (love),
Both build the trajectory of the above.
Habit to cut: only love when burned out or only cash when scared—whiplash calendar—
Half that chaos—steady the dual planar.
Tomorrow: name the imbalance habit, cut half, schedule one love block and protect one cash commitment clean—
Sex-and-cash on the screen of your week as a team.
[Bridge]
Purity myths starve people...
Balance feeds the long game of making...
Money is not the enemy of art—dishonesty is...
Love is not the enemy of money—avoidance is.
[Verse 3 / Outro]
So run sex-and-cash theory—MacLeod's adult creative accounting,
Love and money both counting.
Cut the shame-or-imbalance habit in half this week; write the two columns; fund both with hours,
Ignore Everybody's powers as practical, not only flowers of quote.
When hustle culture says only monetize and bohemian culture says only starve for purity,
Choose MacLeod maturity.
One love deliverable and one cash duty both honored by Friday—proof of balance—
Dual talents.
Love feeds—cash feeds—you steer.
Balance—here.

headline: Keep a day job while building the body of work
song:
Yo, Hugh MacLeod, Ignore Everybody—payroll as patron:
Keep a day job while building the body of work—modern matron of freedom.
The job buys time and calm so the art can stay honest—
Flip it for today, two tracks, no false promise of instant honest riches.
[Chorus]
Keep a day job while building the body of work—oxygen and clay,
Rent paid, brain freer to make what may not pay today.
Body of work is the long stack of finished things with your name,
Day job is the frame that keeps hunger from rewriting the game wrong.
Not forever prison—strategic runway—
Build while you're paid.
MacLeod: don't romanticize starving if it kills the drawing hand—
Day job—stand.
[Verse 1]
MacLeod kept making while life still had structure and income,
The point: financial panic is a terrible art director of the outcome.
Day job done ethically leaves evenings, mornings, lunch edges for the body of work,
Cartoons, pages, code, songs—stack until the work can jerk itself into more support.
Body of work means volume + voice over years, not one viral lottery ticket,
Day job is the quiet ticket to consistency when markets ignore you in the lonely childhood.
History of creators: Kafka at the insurance office mythos, countless designers with clients by day,
The pattern is keep making either way—with a floor under the pay.
Ignore Everybody fights the glamor of "quit first, figure out later" as only path,
Sometimes quitting is right; often building first is the math.
Restate short: keep paycheck, stack originals on the side until they can ride—
Then 25 minutes that obey—no posting—just the side.
Structures of pure hustle culture will shame the day job; ignore that purity structure,
Your body of work is the future.
Job isn't the enemy of art—using the job as identity so you never make is the enemy,
Hold both: employee hours, author enemy of blank.
[Chorus]
[Verse 2]
Modern day apply it: software job by day, indie product nights—body of work in public repos and posts,
Don't quit at first like for ghosts of influencer hosts.
Teacher salary + novel mornings; nurse shifts + illustration weekends—same MacLeod physics,
Runway is a gift if you use it, not only a fix of status.
Gig economy day-job equivalents: stable client retainers while you build the personal brand of craft,
Not only chasing every fad draft.
AI side projects: use day-job skills as leverage, off-hours as the lab,
Body of work compounds like interest when you don't stab the runway for drama.
Twenty-five minutes tonight: one unit of the body—page, panel, feature, lesson—no posting flex,
Only the next.
Tomorrow: twelve-word restate, one block that proves day job + body can coexist without self-hate—
Ignore Everybody's straight gait.
[Bridge]
Starving can be a story you tell to feel pure...
Stability can be a door to make more...
Neither automatic—intention decides...
Day job while the body of work abides.
[Verse 3 / Outro]
So keep a day job while building the body of work—MacLeod's practical courage,
Not a forever cage—a scaffolding for the voyage.
Restate in twelve words, run twenty-five minutes on the body—no posting—
One brick in the loading of your voice.
When quit-lit and guru threads make your stable job look like failure theater,
Ignore that everybody—build neater.
Paycheck Friday, personal finish Sunday—stack for a year—then reassess with data—
Body greater.
Job holds—work grows—you choose the pace.
Build both—claim space.

headline: Be an artist first, entrepreneur second
song:
Yo, Hugh MacLeod, Ignore Everybody—order of operations:
Be an artist first, entrepreneur second—check the foundations.
Make the thing real before you brand the empire around a ghost—
Flip it for today, craft foremost.
[Chorus]
Artist first, entrepreneur second—sequence matters cold,
If the art is weak, the business is a story sold to the old of hype.
Entrepreneur skills help art travel; they don't replace the drawing hand,
First command: make it stand.
MacLeod sold work because the work had a pulse before the funnel,
Art first—then the tunnel of market.
Don't reverse it into empty hustle with a creative costume—
Artist—then business costume.
[Verse 1]
MacLeod watched people fall in love with the identity of "founder/creator brand"
Before they had a body of work that could withstand a hand of real scrutiny.
Artist first means daily practice, finished pieces, a point of view sharpened by reps,
Entrepreneur second means once there's something true, learn to step into distribution and steps of sale.
He was a cartoonist who learned business—not a business deck that hired a cartoonist vibe,
Order is the tribe of integrity.
History of creator economy failures: beautiful Shopify themes around hollow products,
And quiet artists who never learned a second skill and stayed invisible longer than needed—both lessons.
Ignore Everybody wants the work to lead so marketing isn't lying,
Entrepreneurship as amplifier, not substitute for trying.
Sex-and-cash still fits: art is the love core; business is how cash and reach don't corrupt the core if sequenced right,
First light, then the flight of the store.
Seven mornings: read the line, two-minute action matching—make two minutes of art before any growth hack,
Sequence in the stack of the day as fact.
[Chorus]
[Verse 2]
Modern day apply it: before you buy the course on personal brand, finish three real pieces this week,
Artist first—entrepreneur later as technique.
AI makes logo kits and landing pages instant—don't let that invert you into CEO of vapor,
Ship the cartoon, song, tool, essay—paper over the caper of pure funnel.
Startup with "creative" in the deck: still prototype the experience users feel,
Art of product first—pitch second as the wheel.
Musician: write and record before the merch drop fantasy owns the calendar,
Sequence is the manager.
Two minutes after reading MacLeod each morning: one line drawn, one sentence written, one bar hummed—
Business email second if at all in that window—artist drum.
[Bridge]
Hustle without craft is a costume party...
Craft without any enterprise can be a lonely tardy to the world...
First things first keeps both honest...
Art leads—commerce can follow modest or loud later.
[Verse 3 / Outro]
So be an artist first, entrepreneur second—MacLeod's sequence for a clean conscience,
Ignore the inverted nonsense of brand-before-hand.
Seven days: line first, then optionally one entrepreneurial two-minute act—email, price, post the real thing,
Never post the shell of nothing.
When every platform teaches growth loops before taste loops,
Flip the classroom in your room.
Art stacked all week, one honest business move on the strongest piece—order restored—
Artist—board; entrepreneur—aboard second.
Make—then market.
First—the art of it.

headline: Simple bold lines beat committee taste
song:
Yo, Hugh MacLeod, Ignore Everybody—pen on the card:
Simple bold lines beat committee taste—draw hard.
One clear mark over ten diluted compromises in beige—
Flip it for today, bold the page.
[Chorus]
Simple bold lines beat committee taste—clarity wins the fight,
Committees sand edges till nothing can bite.
Bold is a decision; simple is a respect for the eye and the mind,
Committee taste is kind of unkind to the original kind.
MacLeod's cartoons: few lines, full punch, no meeting required to approve the ink,
Simple bold—think.
One voice sharp > many voices mush—
Bold line—trust.
[Verse 1]
MacLeod's literal craft was simple bold lines on business cards—constraint as amplifier,
The metaphor scales to products, brands, essays, apps: fewer stronger marks, higher fire.
Committee taste optimizes to offend no one and therefore move no one,
Every stakeholder gets a pixel; the soul gets none.
He ignored everybody enough to keep the line clean when advice said "add more, soften, explain,"
Simple bold is a restrain and a remain of point of view.
History of design and comedy and advertising that works: ruthless simplicity with courage,
Committees famous for camels designed as horses—MacLeod's warning in plain language.
Good ideas' lonely childhoods need bold lines to stay themselves through growth,
Not a thousand "what if we also" that dilute the oath.
Make one small finished thing that could only exist if simple bold beats committee—
A card, a poster, a feature with one job, a sentence without the apology.
Taste here is personal responsibility: your eye, your cut, your no,
Committee taste is outsourced no-one's-fault woe.
[Chorus]
[Verse 2]
Modern day apply it: product PRD with one job-to-be-done bold at the top—cut the kitchen sink,
Simple line of purpose before the committee of think-tanks blink.
Slide deck: one idea per slide, big type, kill the clip art democracy,
Bold beats the hypocrisy of twelve fonts for "alignment."
AI outputs verbose by default—your art is the bold cut to the bone,
Simple edit as the throne of taste you own.
Brand team of twelve: assign a final decider, not a taste average algorithm of fear,
Committee taste stops here if you make the role clear.
Kid's poster for school: praise the strong simple choice, not the glitter overthink,
Bold lines teach kids to mean it.
Tomorrow: one finished small thing—postcard rule—few elements, strong claim, no group edit first pass—
MacLeod mass in your hands.
[Bridge]
Complexity often hides fear of choosing...
Simple bold is exposing...
Committees hide inside each other...
One line—another way to say brother to your own eye.
[Verse 3 / Outro]
So simple bold lines beat committee taste—prove it with a finished mark today,
MacLeod's card-sized play.
One small thing: ruthless simplicity, your taste unaveraged, ship it to one human,
No committee baptism.
When work tools invite endless comments that beige the bold,
Close the thread—hold the line you sold yourself at the start.
Bold simple artifact done—put it on the wall or in the world before sleep—
Taste—keep.
Few lines—full force.
Beat the committee—of course.

headline: Market after making, not instead of making
song:
Yo, Hugh MacLeod, Ignore Everybody—order of the hustle corrected:
Market after making, not instead of making—get it protected.
Promotion without product is a costume parade in an empty gym—
Flip it for today, make, then hymn the work to them.
[Chorus]
Market after making—not instead—sequence the grind,
If the hours flip, you're a marketer of a missing mind.
Making is the root; marketing is the road the root travels on,
Skip the root and the brand is a con.
MacLeod sold what he drew; he didn't only draw a funnel and call it art,
After—not instead—start.
Schedule making first; marketing earns its turn—
Burn the reverse burn.
[Verse 1]
MacLeod lived the tension every creative with bills knows: you must get found,
But found empty is a sound of hollowness that compounds into cynicism.
Marketing instead of making is endless optimization of bios, hashtags, networking drinks,
While the body of work shrinks.
Marketing after making respects both: you have something true, then you learn to carry it to humans,
Entrepreneur second still matters—after the artist has munitions.
History of creator burnout: people who became full-time self-promoters of a past self,
MacLeod's warning shelf: keep the pen moving or the market becomes a shell game.
Ignore Everybody includes ignoring the pressure to always be "on brand" in public when you should be in private practice,
Make in the phone-free spaces.
Book one phone-free hour titled the insight—show up even if mood disappears—
Making hour sacred; marketing later with clearer eyes and ears.
The metric: this week, did making hours exceed pure market hours until you have new work,
Then market the work, not the persona's smirk.
[Chorus]
[Verse 2]
Modern day apply it: block Instagram scheduled posts until the comic/page/feature exists,
After making—resist the instead twist of empty carousels.
Startup: stop tweaking the waitlist landing page for week three with no product slice,
Make the slice—then market the nice of real.
Open source: code the fix, then tweet the fix—not only the thread about "building in public" with no build,
Public needs the guild of actual output.
Job seeker: portfolio piece before the hundredth LinkedIn polish pass,
Market the proof, not only the glass of personal brand class.
Phone-free hour: title "make first," leave with an artifact; only then open the channel to share—
Mood optional—be there.
Tomorrow: book the hour, protect it from "just one quick promotional task,"
Making is the mask-off task that matters.
[Bridge]
The market rewards signals; empty signals rot...
Making is the plot...
Promotion is a servant; a master it ruins the house...
After making—announce.
[Verse 3 / Outro]
So market after making, not instead of making—MacLeod's anti-hollow rule,
Ignore the everybody who only teaches the cool of distribution school without materials.
Phone-free hour tomorrow—make—then optionally one honest market move with the thing in hand,
Not a sand castle brand.
When algorithms pay pure marketers of air, still choose the slower stack of real—
Make the meal, then ring the bell.
Artifact first, audience second in the day order—keep the vow—
After—now.
Make—then market—never the reverse as default.
MacLeod—result.

headline: Avoid designing by committee
song:
Yo, Hugh MacLeod, Ignore Everybody—death by roundtable:
Avoid designing by committee—kill the unstable average of taste.
Too many hands on the wheel and the car parks in the middle of nowhere useful—
Flip it for today, one driver, advisors optional and truthful.
[Chorus]
Avoid designing by committee—one clear owner of the cut,
Advice welcome; decision rights not a free-for-all glut.
Committees turn bold lines into polite fog by lunch,
Design dies in the bunch.
MacLeod's simple bold can't survive twelve equal vetoes,
Avoid the heroes of consensus who are zeros for vision.
Owner decides, group informs—keep that law—
Committee draw—withdraw.
[Verse 1]
MacLeod's whole aesthetic is anti-committee: personal, quick, undeniable on a small card,
A committee would have asked for more shading, more logos, more "on brand" hard.
Designing by committee isn't collaboration—it's diffused responsibility with diluted outcomes,
Everyone slightly unhappy, no one accountable when it bottoms.
Collaboration with a decider is healthy; committee as decider is a blender on puree,
Ignore Everybody when the room only purees.
History of bad products and bad campaigns: stakeholder pile-on without hierarchy of taste,
Frankensteins ship and users waste their haste.
Godin and MacLeod rhyme here from different angles—permission and ignore—
Both hate work that only exists to survive a meeting's floor.
Seven mornings: read the line, two-minute act—remove one unnecessary approver from a path,
Or claim decision rights on a small design you own in the math of your job.
Avoid means structure your process: timebox feedback, single synthesizer, ship date sacred,
Committee is a habit, not a fate, unless you let it be nested as default sacred.
[Chorus]
[Verse 2]
Modern day apply it: Figma file with thirty commenters and no owner—assign the owner today,
Avoid committee by role, not only by essay.
Wedding planning, syllabus design, app onboarding—same disease in civilian clothes,
One taste captain, others as scouts, not equal votes on every rose.
AI committee of tools—Chat this, Claude that, Midjourney vote—still need one human final line,
Avoid designing by model committee without a spine.
Slack "quick thoughts everyone?" on a logo is a trap—
Private three notes max, then the designer snaps the map closed.
Two minutes each morning: decline one drive-by opinion loop or consolidate feedback into one digest you control,
Committee patrol canceled.
[Bridge]
Many eyes can help see; many hands shouldn't steer...
Ownership is clear or the work disappears into the near of beige...
Feedback is fuel; veto crowds are theft...
Leave committee design on the shelf.
[Verse 3 / Outro]
So avoid designing by committee—MacLeod's process hygiene for bold work,
Ignore Everybody's smirk at the meeting that never ships the quirk.
Seven days: read, then two minutes of anti-committee action—claim, cut approvers, close the loop,
Design with a group, decide as a troop of one accountable.
When workplaces worship alignment as endless averaging,
Be the person who writes "decider: me, input by Friday" on the calendar of making.
One project this week freed from committee mush—ship it sharper—
Avoid—barter nothing for that edge.
One driver—many scouts—no blender.
Committee off—enter.

headline: Persist through obscurity
song:
Yo, Hugh MacLeod, Ignore Everybody—long hallway with no lights yet:
Persist through obscurity—don't need the internet's yes to sweat.
Nobody knows your name; keep stacking the work anyway—
Flip it for today, obscure is a phase, not a stay-or-quit day.
[Chorus]
Persist through obscurity—lonely childhood stretched in years,
Silence isn't always a verdict—sometimes it's the gears still shifting under the cheers that haven't arrived.
Obscurity is the tax on original; payment is continued making,
Not a reason for forsaking.
MacLeod drew when the room was empty of fame—
Persist—same.
Show up in the dark so there's something to light later—
Obscure—greater if you stay.
[Verse 1]
MacLeod's career arc includes long stretches where the work mattered more than the metrics,
Obscurity is default for anything not yet lucky or networked or timed to the aesthetics of the moment.
Persist means calendar, not mood: another card, another post, another page when zero claps,
The body of work is built in the gaps of attention maps.
Ignore Everybody helps here—because obscurity hurts more when you listen to everybody's highlight reels,
Selective ignore keeps the wheels from comparing wounds to their ideal steels.
History of nearly every lasting maker includes a forgotten basement era,
Romanticized later, miserable then—persistence is the era's hero, not the myth's camera.
Obscurity isn't proof you're bad; visibility isn't proof you're done growing,
But quitting in obscurity guarantees the story stops mid-flowing.
Book phone-free hour titled the insight—show up even if mood disappears—
Obscurity practice: make without an audience near.
The only way out of obscure is through a pile of finished attempts the future can discover,
Persist is the mother of that cover.
[Chorus]
[Verse 2]
Modern day apply it: newsletter at forty subscribers—write the next one like forty thousand might need it someday,
Persist the quality, not the despair of the count's gray.
Open-source lib with three stars—keep the README honest and the commits clean,
Obscurity with craft is still a scene of integrity.
Local musician, neighborhood teacher with a method, parent-blogger at zero viral—
Same MacLeod spiral: keep the work viral-proof by being real.
AI noise makes the feed louder and you smaller-feeling—persist offline blocks harder,
Phone-free hour as the armor.
Tomorrow: book the hour, title "obscurity gym," produce one full unit nobody may see this week—
And still complete—technique.
[Bridge]
Fame is weather; practice is climate...
Obscurity tests whether you meant the art or the spotlight's private...
Stay because the work is true...
Not only because someone clapped for you.
[Verse 3 / Outro]
So persist through obscurity—MacLeod's stamina sermon without the sermon tone,
Ignore Everybody when the tone of the feed says you're alone and therefore wrong.
Phone-free hour tomorrow—mood optional—finish something for the stack,
Obscurity doesn't get to cancel the pact.
When culture only platform-celebrates the already-bright, be bright in the basement on purpose—
Later light needs a surface.
One more finished piece in the dark—that's the win that compounds—
Persist—responds to silence with product.
Stack in the dark—dawn is slow.
Obscure—still go.

headline: Your work is your path
song:
Yo, Hugh MacLeod, Ignore Everybody—last station, clear map:
Your work is your path—not the applause app.
The road is the making itself; destination myths can wait—
Flip it for today, walk the slate of daily create.
[Chorus]
Your work is your path—steps are sketches, commits, pages, calls,
Not a ladder someone else installs.
Path means direction revealed by doing, not by endless planning cosplay,
Work shows the way.
MacLeod: keep drawing and the life arranges around the hand in motion,
Path is the devotion.
Stop waiting for the perfect role to begin the trail—
Work—is—the rail.
[Verse 1]
MacLeod closes the philosophy: identity and direction come from the pile of what you've made,
Not from job titles alone or from being told you made the grade by a stranger's blade of taste.
Your work is your path means the cartoons, the products, the lessons, the code—each is a stepping stone you can stand on,
Portfolio as autobiography of action.
Ignore Everybody so the path isn't rerouted by every tourist's opinion of your hike,
Selective ears, bold lines, lonely ideas, day job runway—all the kit for the like of a long walk.
History of careers that look intentional in reverse were often just people who kept working forward,
Path appears in the rearview more than the brochure.
Sex-and-cash, artist-first, persist through obscure—all feed this headline:
The path is paved with shipped time, not with wish-lines.
Seven mornings: read MacLeod's line, two-minute matching action on the actual path—make, don't only map,
Two minutes of work is two minutes of path, clap or no clap.
When people say "find your passion" as if it's a hidden lake, MacLeod says cut a trail with tools,
Work reveals the rules of you.
[Chorus]
[Verse 2]
Modern day apply it: career confusion? Build three small finished works this month in directions you're curious,
Path data > another personality quiz furious with false precision.
AI era: your path is your taste and your shipped judgment under your name,
Not the model that can fake the same surface game.
Student choosing majors: projects over pure prestige signaling—path is portfolio,
Work that teaches what you actually want to do tomorrow.
Stuck employee: path might be the side body of work that becomes the main, or the craft inside the job made more artful,
Either way—work is the cartful of evidence.
Two minutes daily after the line: one brick—sentence, sketch, refactor, outreach that is the work itself—
Path is the shelf filling.
[Bridge]
Maps without walking are posters...
Walking without making is still lost for a maker...
Your path is under your hands...
Not only in plans.
[Verse 3 / Outro]
So your work is your path—MacLeod's send-off and the book's spine in one line,
Ignore Everybody, keep the work as the shrine and the road combined.
Seven mornings: read, two minutes of real making—own the path under construction,
No waiting for permission or perfect instructions.
When feeds sell destinies and gurus sell secret maps for a fee,
Pay with hours of work for free of illusion—path is the tuition.
Tomorrow's two minutes start the next mile—then the next—until the body of work is a highway—
Your work—your way.
Path under hands—walk it daily.
Make—the trail—really.
